core_thesis: Harry Miller is a New York-based poet and personal trainer who fuses physical discipline, spoken word, and spiritual philosophy into a unified practice of self-overcoming. His central argument is that wisdom, poetry, and bodily strength are all forms of warfare — tools for confronting inner demons, societal indifference, and existential despair. He channels personal experience with depression and suicide into a public advocacy posture, positioning resilience as prophetic and the struggle itself as the path to victory.
key_ideas: Mental health advocacy and suicide prevention as a public, prophetic mission
Wisdom as warfare — life as active spiritual combat requiring courage and presence
Poetry and spoken word as instruments of personal and collective transformation
Physical training as a parallel and complementary discipline to mental and spiritual strength
Prophetic identity — the visionary dismissed by contemporaries who is ultimately vindicated
Nature and communal presence as spiritual practice (Sermon of the Earth framework)
Philosophical aphorism-making and dark humor as coping and meaning-making tools
Community-building through milestones, events, and shared witness
